policy: {"applicabilityConditions":["Azure AI infrastructure requires material sequential investment","Scaled capital investment would increase COGS","FY24 operating-expense growth is expected to remain low","Capacity buildout timing varies by quarter"],"confidence":"medium","contrastingEventIds":["event-cloud-capacity-scale-2022"],"directPolicyEvidenceIds":[],"domain":"ai_cloud_capex","evidenceIds":["span-capacity-2022-capex","span-capacity-2022-headcount","span-capacity-2023-ai-capex","span-capacity-2023-opex","span-capacity-2024-demand-discipline","span-capacity-2024-external-supply"],"exceptions":["customer demand guides investment","disciplined cost management across every team","external supply supplements owned capacity when lead time requires it"],"id":"policy-c4203c075dbd61d3","nonApplicabilityConditions":["global cloud-services demand was growing","investment needs spanned infrastructure and multiple operating teams"],"priorityOrder":["customer demand","capacity urgency","profitability or cost constraint","workforce productivity","supply lead time"],"recommendedAction":"scale_infrastructure_constrain_opex","reversalSignals":["weakening demand or pipeline","relaxed capacity urgency","changed infrastructure economics"],"supportingEventIds":["event-ai-capacity-opex-pivot-2023","event-ai-capacity-sourcing-2024"]}
reflections: {"actionDelta":"2022 expanded both infrastructure and people; 2023-2024 scaled infrastructure while constraining operating expense growth.","boundaryConditions":["customer demand guides investment","disciplined cost management across every team","external supply supplements owned capacity when lead time requires"],"conditionDelta":"2022 was broad cloud demand across multiple areas; 2023-2024 was a concentrated AI platform shift with profitability pressure.","confidence":"medium","contrastingEventIds":["event-cloud-capacity-scale-2022"],"contrastPattern":{"action":"scale_infrastructure_and_people","conditions":["global cloud-services demand was growing","investment needs spanned infrastructure and multiple operating teams"],"eventIds":["event-cloud-capacity-scale-2022"],"evidenceIds":["span-capacity-2022-capex","span-capacity-2022-headcount"]},"crossEventQuestion":"How should Microsoft balance infrastructure investment against operating expense growth during a platform shift?","decisionAxis":{"choiceSet":["scale_infrastructure_and_people","scale_infrastructure_constrain_opex"],"decisionObject":"resource allocation","decisionQuestion":"scale_infrastructure_and_people vs scale_infrastructure_constrain_opex","gatingVariables":["demand type","platform shift","profitability pressure"]},"domain":"ai_cloud_capex","evidenceIds":["span-capacity-2022-capex","span-capacity-2022-headcount","span-capacity-2023-ai-capex","span-capacity-2023-opex","span-capacity-2024-demand-discipline","span-capacity-2024-external-supply"],"id":"reflection-4b5f7915d30581a4","invariant":"During a platform shift (AI), capital should be concentrated on infrastructure capacity while operating resources are reallocated to growth areas.","observation":"In 2022, Microsoft scaled both infrastructure and headcount (20% YoY) under broad cloud demand; events: {"candidateId":"candidate-ai-capacity-opex-pivot-2023","chosenAction":"scale_infrastructure_constrain_opex","conditions":["Azure AI infrastructure required material sequential investment.","Scaled capital investment would increase COGS."],"constraints":["FY24 operating-expense growth was expected to remain low.","Capacity buildout timing varies by quarter."],"decisionDate":"2023-04-25","domain":"ai_cloud_capex","evidenceIds":["span-capacity-2023-ai-capex","span-capacity-2023-opex"],"id":"event-ai-capacity-opex-pivot-2023","objectives":["Lead the AI platform wave","Maintain healthy profitability while scaling capacity"],"rejectedBenefit":"Expanding people with infrastructure could reduce execution bottlenecks.","situation":"Azure AI infrastructure required a material capacity increase while Microsoft was aligning costs with revenue growth and protecting profitability.","title":"Scale Azure AI infrastructure while constraining operating expense"}

{"candidateId":"candidate-ai-capacity-sourcing-2024","chosenAction":"scale_infrastructure_constrain_opex","conditions":["Customer demand guided cloud and AI investment.","Third-party capacity supplemented owned capacity when lead time required it."],"constraints":["AI capital investment increases COGS.","Every team remained subject to disciplined cost management."],"decisionDate":"2024-01-30","domain":"ai_cloud_capex","evidenceIds":["span-capacity-2024-demand-discipline","span-capacity-2024-external-supply"],"id":"event-ai-capacity-sourcing-2024","objectives":["Add capacity in time to serve visible customer demand","Preserve operating-margin discipline while scaling AI investment"],"rejectedBenefit":"Scaling people alongside owned capacity could increase direct control and reduce external dependency.","situation":"Customer demand and pipeline required faster cloud and AI capacity additions than owned buildout alone could always deliver.","title":"Accelerate demand-led AI capacity with disciplined cost and mixed supply"}
